concepts.title: Call Delegation (Shared Line Appearance)
concepts.summary: A feature that allows phone users to assign assistants or colleagues to handle calls on their behalf, also known as shared line appearance. From a UX perspective, delegation creates a dual-role relationship where delegates can answer, place, and manage calls for the delegator, requiring clear visual indicators to distinguish between acting on one's own behalf versus on behalf of another user.
key_points: Enables phone users to designate others to make and receive calls on their behalf
Functions at multiple hierarchy levels: Account, Group, Site, Phone User, and Common Area
Requires enabling by an admin before users can set up delegation relationships

concepts.title: Delegation Toggle
concepts.summary: A simple enable/disable toggle for the delegation feature, consistently used across all hierarchy levels (Account, Group, Site, Phone User, Common Area). The toggle is the primary interaction for controlling delegation availability, and its consistent placement and behavior across levels reduces admin learning curve.
key_points: Same toggle interaction across all five hierarchy levels
Requires verification dialog confirmation on change
Grayed-out state indicates the setting is locked at a higher hierarchy level
Optional lock icon allows enforcement at current level to prevent lower-level override

concepts.title: Hierarchical Settings Lock
concepts.summary: A governance mechanism where settings configured at a higher hierarchy level (Account) propagate down and can be locked to prevent changes at lower levels (Group, Site, User). When locked, lower-level toggles appear grayed out with a note directing admins to change the setting at the locking level. This pattern is foundational to Zoom Phone's admin UX and must clearly communicate the lock source to avoid confusion.
key_points: Account > Group/Site > Phone User/Common Area hierarchy
Lock icon enforces the current setting and prevents lower-level changes
Grayed-out toggle with note indicates the setting is locked at a higher level
Admins must navigate to the locking level to make changes

user_roles.title: Account Owner / Admin (Delegation)
user_roles.summary: Enables or disables the call delegation feature at any hierarchy level. Must have specific privilege scopes depending on the level being configured: account settings for Account level, group management for Group level, and site management for Site level. The UI must clearly indicate which privilege is needed at each level and provide appropriate access controls.
key_points: Account level: requires privilege to edit account settings
Group level: requires privilege to edit groups
Site level: requires privilege to edit sites
Can lock settings to prevent lower-level overrides
Manages delegation through different navigation paths depending on hierarchy level

task_flows.title: Enable/Disable Delegation at Account Level
task_flows.summary: The top-level control for delegation that affects all users in the account. When locked, this setting becomes the single source of truth, and all lower levels are forced to comply. Admins access this through Account Management > Account Settings > Zoom Phone, emphasizing that delegation is a phone-specific feature.
key_points: Navigate to Account Management > Account Settings > Zoom Phone tab
Toggle Delegation under the General section
Confirm change via verification dialog
Optional: lock to prevent group/site/user level changes

task_flows.title: Enable/Disable Delegation at Group Level
task_flows.summary: Allows admins to control delegation for a specific user group. If the account-level setting is locked, the group toggle appears grayed out. The navigation path (User Management > Groups) differs from the account path, which could cause confusion -- designers should consider consistent navigation patterns for settings that span hierarchy levels.
key_points: Navigate to User Management > Groups > select group > Zoom Phone tab
Toggle Delegation under the General section
Grayed out if locked at account level
Optional: lock to prevent user-level changes within the group

task_flows.title: Enable/Disable Delegation at Site Level
task_flows.summary: Controls delegation for users within a specific site. The navigation path (Phone System Management > Company Info > Policy tab) differs from both Account and Group paths, introducing a third admin navigation pattern for the same feature. Consistent Policy tab placement at the Site level provides some predictability.
key_points: Navigate to Phone System Management > Company Info > select site > Policy tab
Toggle Delegation under the General section
Grayed out if locked at account level
Optional: lock to prevent user-level changes within the site

task_flows.title: Enable/Disable Delegation at Phone User Level
task_flows.summary: The most granular delegation control, applied to individual phone users. Includes an additional option not present at higher levels: 'Restrict from adding delegation to users outside their own site', which limits cross-site delegation. This site-boundary constraint is a privacy/governance feature that only appears at the user level and should be clearly explained.
key_points: Navigate to Phone System Management > Users & Rooms > Users tab > select user > Policy tab
Toggle Delegation under the General section
Additional option: Restrict from adding delegation to users outside their own site (checkbox)
Verification dialog confirms each change

task_flows.title: Enable/Disable Delegation at Common Area Level
task_flows.summary: Controls delegation for common area phones (shared devices like lobby or conference room phones). The flow mirrors the phone user level but with a simplified set of options (no site-restriction checkbox). Navigation is through the Common Areas tab next to Users under Users & Rooms, providing a parallel but distinct path.
key_points: Navigate to Phone System Management > Users & Rooms > Common Areas tab > select common area > Policy tab
Toggle Delegation under the General section
No site-restriction option (unlike phone user level)
Changes apply only to the selected common area

constraints.title: Hierarchy Lock Override Rule
constraints.summary: When a setting is locked at a higher hierarchy level (e.g., Account), lower-level toggles are grayed out and cannot be changed. Admins must navigate to the locking level to modify the setting. This constraint is communicated through UI state (grayed out toggle + informational note) rather than blocking navigation, which preserves exploration while preventing changes.
key_points: Account-level lock overrides all lower levels
Grayed-out toggle indicates the setting is locked upstream
Informational note directs admin to the locking level

constraints.title: Admin Privilege Required
constraints.summary: Only account owners or admins with appropriate privileges can enable or disable delegation. Different hierarchy levels require different privilege scopes (edit account settings, edit groups, edit sites), which means the same admin may have access at some levels but not others.
key_points: Account owner or admin privileges required at all levels
Group level requires privilege to edit groups
Site level requires privilege to edit sites
Account level requires privilege to edit account settings

constraints.title: License Requirement
constraints.summary: Enabling delegation requires either a Zoom Workplace license with Phone included or a standalone Zoom Phone calling plan. This prerequisite should be validated before showing the delegation toggle to avoid exposing non-functional UI.
key_points: Requires Zoom Workplace license with Phone included, or standalone Zoom Phone calling plan

ux_patterns.title: Hierarchical Settings with Lock Propagation
ux_patterns.summary: A governance pattern where settings cascade from Account > Group/Site > Phone User/Common Area, with lock mechanisms at each level to prevent downstream overrides. This pattern is reusable across all Zoom Phone policy settings and should use consistent visual language (toggle, lock icon, grayed-out disabled state) to build admin familiarity.
key_points: Top-down cascade: Account overrides Group/Site, which override Phone User/Common Area
Lock icon at each level enforces the current setting downward
Grayed-out state + informational note communicates upstream lock
Consistent toggle component across all five levels

ux_patterns.title: Verification Dialog on Toggle
ux_patterns.summary: When toggling delegation on or off, a verification dialog appears requiring explicit confirmation. This pattern prevents accidental changes to settings that have broad downstream impact, particularly important for Account and Group level changes that affect many users.
key_points: Appears after toggling delegation at any level
Requires clicking Enable or Disable to confirm the change
Acts as a safety net for high-impact settings changes

ux_patterns.title: Policy Tab for Feature-Specific Settings
ux_patterns.summary: At the Site, Phone User, and Common Area levels, delegation settings are located under a Policy tab rather than a general settings tab. This separation of policy controls from other configuration creates a predictable location for governance-related settings across different entity types.
key_points: Policy tab appears at Site, Phone User, and Common Area levels
Separates governance/compliance settings from general configuration
Account and Group levels use Zoom Phone tab instead, creating an inconsistency worth noting
